content: Language education's explicit vocabulary teaching of 'reflection' as both a physical concept (light reflection) and metacognitive practice can be repurposed to design better LLM self-reflection mechanisms, by grounding abstract self-improvement steps in concrete, multi-sense semantic anchors. Just as students learn reflection through linking its dual meanings, LLMs can be fine-tuned to map self-correction to the physical logic of light bouncing back to its source to reveal hidden obstacles.
